title: Classes - a thing that holds its own state
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A class is a blueprint. __init__ sets up each new object's data; methods are functions that belong to it and act on self.
explanation: When a bundle of data and the things you do to it keep travelling together, a class names that bundle. __init__ runs when you make one, storing values on self. A method like deposit() reaches into self to change it. self is just 'this particular object' - the one the method was called on.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: n starts at 0 when the Counter is made.
Each tick() adds one to self.n.

exercises.question: Which method runs automatically when you create a new object?
hints: The one with the double underscores, for 'initialize'.
It's the first parameter of every method.
